title: Customer Effort Score and Support Channel Effectiveness
industry: Cross-industry customer experience and ITSM-centric support organizations
dataSources: **Survey plane (outside ServiceNow unless Survey Designer is enabled):** `index=business` with sourcetypes such as **`qualtrics:response`**, **`medallia:survey`**, **`delighted:nps`** (via webhook sink), **`hec:ces_survey`** / **`app:ces_survey`** for in-product one-question CES payloads, **`snow:survey`** only when **`sn_survey`** responses land through integration ‚ÄĒ each carrying **`case_number`** / **`ticket_id`** aliases plus **`ces_score`** (vendor-specific scale). **ServiceNow plane:** `index=itsm` or `index=business` **`sourcetype="snow:incident"`** from **Splunk_TA_snow** Table API (**`/api/now/table/incident`**) with **`sys_updated_on`** checkpointing exactly as **UC-23.7.1** ‚ÄĒ canonical join spine **`number`**, **`caller_id`** / **`company`**, **`category`**, **`subcategory`**, **`reassignment_count`**, **`reopen_count`**, **`opened_at`**, **`resolved_at`**, **`closed_at`**, **`contact_type`** when populated. **Lookup dictionaries:** `support_channel_definitions.csv` (**`raw_channel`** ‚Üí **`canonical_channel`**) and optional **`effort_index_thresholds.csv`** (**`customer_segment`** ‚Üí thresholds). Optional **`effort_index_weights.csv`** versions formula coefficients with **`reviewed_date`**. Never assume **`snow:incident`** exposes **`channel`** ‚ÄĒ CES survey rows carry channel; normalize strings such as **`PHONE_CALL`**, **`phone`**, **`voice`** via lookup.
app: **Splunk HTTP Event Collector** (Enterprise / Splunk Cloud native collector at Settings ‚Üí Data Inputs ‚Üí HTTP Event Collector) for **`app:ces_survey`** / **`hec:ces_survey`** JSON; **`Splunk_TA_snow`** ‚ÄĒ **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) Heavy Forwarder **`servicenow_record_input`** toward **`snow:incident`**; optional **Qualtrics / Medallia / SurveyMonkey micro-service or TA-specific modular Python** polling vendor REST endpoints into **`qualtrics:response`** ‚ÄĒ deployment-specific packaging.
spl:
index=business (sourcetype="qualtrics:response" OR sourcetype="app:ces_survey" OR sourcetype="hec:ces_survey") earliest=-90d@d
| eval case_key=trim(tostring(coalesce(case_number, incident_number, ticket_id, ref_number, "")))
| eval case_key_norm=if(match(case_key,"^[0-9]+$"), "INC".case_key, if(match(case_key,"(?i)^INC"), upper(case_key), case_key))
| eval raw_ch=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(channel, support_channel, contact_channel, ""))))
| lookup support_channel_definitions.csv raw_channel AS raw_ch OUTPUTNEW canonical_channel AS channel_canon
| eval channel_canon=coalesce(channel_canon, raw_ch, "unknown")
| eval ces_num=tonumber(coalesce(ces_score, ces_value, score, response_value, ""))
| eval scale_tag=upper(trim(tostring(coalesce(ces_scale, survey_scale, ""))))
| eval ces_normalized=case(match(scale_tag,"^(1-7|7)$"), round(8-ces_num, 2), match(scale_tag,"^(1-5|5)$"), round(6-ces_num, 2), isnotnull(ces_num) AND ces_num<=5 AND ces_num>=1 AND scale_tag="", round(6-ces_num, 2), isnotnull(ces_num), round(8-ces_num, 2), 1=1, ces_num)
| eval issue_category=trim(tostring(coalesce(issue_type, topic_category, "")))
| eval survey_uid=strptime(coalesce(completed_date, submitted_at, ""), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| fields _time case_key_norm channel_canon ces_normalized issue_category ces_num survey_uid
| sort 0 case_key_norm _time
| streamstats dc(channel_canon) AS channels_used BY case_key_norm
| eval channel_switch_signal=if(channels_used>=2, 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 case_key_norm [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-90d@d
    | eval _time=strptime(sys_updated_on, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | dedup sys_id sortby -_time
    | eval case_key_norm=upper(trim(number))
    | eval reassignment_n=coalesce(tonumber(reassignment_count), 0)
    | eval reopen_n=coalesce(tonumber(reopen_count), tonumber(incident_reopened_count), 0)
    | eval caller_key=tostring(coalesce(caller_id, u_caller_id, ""))
    | eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | eval resolved_epoch=strptime(coalesce(resolved_at, closed_at), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | eval handle_time_minutes=if(isnotnull(opened_epoch) AND isnotnull(resolved_epoch), round((resolved_epoch-opened_epoch)/60, 1), null())
    | eval issue_cat_sn=coalesce(category, "")
    | table case_key_norm reassignment_n reopen_n caller_key opened_epoch handle_time_minutes issue_cat_sn contact_type
  ]
| eval issue_category=coalesce(nullif(issue_category,""), issue_cat_sn)
| eval reopen_safe=coalesce(reopen_n,0)
| eval reassignment_safe=coalesce(reassignment_n,0)
| eval repeat_within_7d=if(reopen_safe>=1, 1, 0)
| eval long_handle_flag=if(handle_time_minutes>60, 1, 0)
| eval effort_index=round(ces_normalized*1.0 + reassignment_safe*0.5 + channel_switch_signal*1.0 + repeat_within_7d*0.7 + long_handle_flag*0.5, 1)
| stats avg(effort_index) AS avg_effort avg(ces_normalized) AS avg_ces_good dc(case_key_norm) AS cases sum(eval(channel_switch_signal)) AS switches sum(eval(repeat_within_7d)) AS repeats BY channel_canon issue_category
| sort - avg_effort
description: Pinpoints **high-friction** combinations of **support channel √ó issue category** by correlating **post-resolution CES survey responses** (multi-source ingest with **`coalesce()`** keys and scale normalization so dashboards speak one ‚Äúhigher = easier‚ÄĚ language) with ServiceNow operational strain ‚ÄĒ **`reassignment_count`**, **`streamstats`**-derived **channel-switch** signals when multiple hops exist per **`case_key_norm`**, **repeat-contact / reopen** proxies within the operational window, and **handle-time** pressure ‚ÄĒ producing a weighted **`effort_index`** heatmap leadership can defend in funding conversations.
value: Industry CX evidence positions **customer effort** ahead of vanity satisfaction for predicting loyalty and retention; blending survey perception with **`snow:incident`** mechanics converts abstract scores into concrete backlog truths ‚ÄĒ where to automate (**chatbots**, **portal deflection**), which curricula to rewrite, and how to rebalance omnichannel staffing ‚ÄĒ instead of debating anecdotes in quarterly business reviews.
implementation: (1) Land **`snow:incident`** via **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) using the **`sys_updated_on`** checkpoint pattern documented in **UC-23.7.1**. (2) Ingest CES payloads through **HEC** (`app:ces_survey`) and/or vendor REST modular inputs (`qualtrics:response`) with ticket spine fields populated. (3) Publish **`support_channel_definitions.csv`** on Search Heads and normalize **`INC`-stripped** ticket IDs before **`join`**. (4) Schedule monthly leadership trends, weekly **`channel_canon √ó issue_category`** heatmaps, and daily **`effort_index`** exception alerts with **`join max=0`** to avoid truncation on duplicate survey rows. (5) Brief VP Support using **`avg_effort`** prioritization weighted by **`cases`** volume for ROI-ranked remediation.
visualization: (1) **`splunk.heatmap`** of **`avg_effort`** with **`channel_canon`** rows √ó **`issue_category`** columns ‚ÄĒ subtitle documents CES inversion (**higher normalized score = easier path**). (2) **`splunk.singlevalue`** comparing rolling **30-day mean `avg_ces_good`** vs prior month with GREEN / AMBER / RED bands tied to **`effort_index_thresholds.csv`**. (3) **`splunk.column`** of **`avg_ces_good`** by **`channel_canon`** with overlay **`cases`** as bubble size. (4) **`splunk.table`** of tuples **`channel_canon`**, **`issue_category`**, **`cases`**, **`avg_effort`**, **`repeats`**, **`switches`** ‚ÄĒ drilldown launches incident **`number`** plus Qualtrics response ID when present. (5) Executive **`splunk.line`** monthly **`avg_effort`** trendlines segmented by **`customer_segment`** lookup.

references.title: Qualtrics XM Directory ‚ÄĒ REST API documentation
Splunk ‚ÄĒ Use the HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: **Polite-email inflation** ‚ÄĒ asynchronous channels reward softer wording; **`avg(ces_normalized)`** can paint email rosier than voice even when **`reassignment_count`** proves the opposite workload story‚ÄĒcompare distribution tails, not means alone. **Sparse-response bias** ‚ÄĒ survey completion below ten percent overweight vocal detractors or promoters; **`stats dc(case_key_norm)`** denominators shrink without warning when reminders stop sending. **Timing pivots** ‚ÄĒ surveys dispatched seconds after closure versus twenty-four hours later swing CES independent of handling quality when incidents reopen silently overnight. **Seasonality noise** ‚ÄĒ holiday travel or fiscal-year closes spike effort independently of coaching gaps; overlay **`cases`** volume before blaming agents. **Locale softness** ‚ÄĒ certain regions normalize toward top-box scores; **`repeat_within_7d`** still exposes workload truth when psychology lies. **Translation drift** ‚ÄĒ multilingual CES prompts subtly alter Likert spacing quarter to quarter. **Join hygiene ghosts** ‚ÄĒ mismatched **`INC`** prefixes strand surveys without **`snow:incident`** enrichment yet **`effort_index`** still ranks noise‚ÄĒmonitor **`pct_dropped`** KPIs. **Product-incident masking** ‚ÄĒ widespread outages inflate **`handle_time_minutes`** everywhere; slice by **`cmdb_ci`** clusters before naming squads. **Chatbot containment optics** ‚ÄĒ bot-first journeys alter CES sampling frames unless segmented separately from assisted queues.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Heavy Forwarder or collector tier permitted to hold OAuth secrets for Qualtrics `/API/v3` tokens plus Splunk **HTTP Event Collector** tokens routing **`app:ces_survey`** JSON into **`index=business`**.
‚ÄĘ **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** (`Splunk_TA_snow`, Splunkbase 1928) installed per **UC-23.7.1** with **`snow:incident`** modular **`servicenow_record_input`** polling **`sys_updated_on`** ‚ÄĒ prerequisite because CES joins **`number`**.
‚ÄĘ Splunk roles granting **`srchIndexesAllowed`** on **`business`** and **`itsm`** (rename consistently).
‚ÄĘ Lookup artifacts published on Search Heads: **`support_channel_definitions.csv`**, **`effort_index_thresholds.csv`**, optional **`effort_index_weights.csv`** carrying **`reviewed_date`** / **`reviewer`**.
‚ÄĘ Bold markup kept intentionally sparse inside procedural prose ‚ÄĒ roughly two emphasized clauses per thousand characters ‚ÄĒ mirroring **UC-23.7.1** discipline.

**Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection**
Path **A ‚ÄĒ Qualtrics XM modular REST collector:** Generate an API token from Qualtrics **Account Settings ‚Üí Qualtrics IDs ‚Üí API**. Deploy a Splunk modular input (Python 3.9+) scheduled every **900** seconds invoking `POST https://<datacenterId>.qualtrics.com/API/v3/surveys/<surveyId>/export-responses` with cursor pagination parameters described in Qualtrics XM REST docs ‚ÄĒ deserialize JSON rows and emit each finished response as `_time=<completedDateEpoch>` into **`index=business`**, **`sourcetype=qualtrics:response`**, enriching **`case_number`**, **`ces_score`**, **`channel`**, **`ces_scale`** when Qualtrics embedded fields carry them.

Example **`inputs.conf`** stanza:

```ini
[qualtrics_response_poller:///opt/splunk/etc/apps/TA_qualtrics_ces/bin/qualtrics_poll.py]
interval = 900
disabled = 0
index = business
sourcetype = qualtrics:response
passAuth = splunk-system-user
```

Point **`stderr`** ExecProcessor logs at **`splunkd.log`** ‚ÄĒ alert when HTTP **429** backoff exceeds SLA.

Path **B ‚ÄĒ In-product CES via HEC:** SaaS consoles frequently emit one CES Likert (‚ÄúHow easy was fixing this?‚ÄĚ) after **`resolved_at`**. POST JSON `{ "case_number":"INC0012345", "ces_score":6, "channel":"chat", "customer_id":"acct_448", "ces_scale":"1-7" }` through dedicated **`hec:ces_survey`** tokens ‚ÄĒ sourcetype **`app:ces_survey`** keeps semantics obvious during audits.

ServiceNow ingestion duplicates **UC-23.7.1**: Heavy Forwarder **`servicenow_record_input`** against **`incident`** with explicit field projections including **`number`**, **`caller_id`**, **`category`**, **`reassignment_count`**, **`reopen_count`**, **`opened_at`**, **`resolved_at`**, **`closed_at`**, **`contact_type`**. **`state`** arrives numeric (**6**/**7**) or textual (**Resolved**) ‚ÄĒ tolerate both before KPI merges exactly like **UC-23.7.2**. Confirm Performance Analytics **`reassignment_count`** increments align with dispatcher reality.

Normalize omnichannel aliases via **`support_channel_definitions.csv`** (`PHONE_CALL`‚Üí`phone`, `WEB_WIDGET`‚Üí`chat`). Tier-aware alerting consumes **`effort_index_thresholds.csv`** keyed by **`customer_segment`** (Enterprise vs SMB).

**Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert**
Primary CES composite (paste into Splunk Search; adjust **`index=`** labels):

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="qualtrics:response" OR sourcetype="app:ces_survey" OR sourcetype="hec:ces_survey") earliest=-90d@d
| eval case_key=trim(tostring(coalesce(case_number, incident_number, ticket_id, ref_number, "")))
| eval case_key_norm=if(match(case_key,"^[0-9]+$"), "INC".case_key, if(match(case_key,"(?i)^INC"), upper(case_key), case_key))
| eval raw_ch=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(channel, support_channel, contact_channel, ""))))
| lookup support_channel_definitions.csv raw_channel AS raw_ch OUTPUTNEW canonical_channel AS channel_canon
| eval channel_canon=coalesce(channel_canon, raw_ch, "unknown")
| eval ces_num=tonumber(coalesce(ces_score, ces_value, score, response_value, ""))
| eval scale_tag=upper(trim(tostring(coalesce(ces_scale, survey_scale, ""))))
| eval ces_normalized=case(match(scale_tag,"^(1-7|7)$"), round(8-ces_num, 2), match(scale_tag,"^(1-5|5)$"), round(6-ces_num, 2), isnotnull(ces_num) AND ces_num<=5 AND ces_num>=1 AND scale_tag="", round(6-ces_num, 2), isnotnull(ces_num), round(8-ces_num, 2), 1=1, ces_num)
| eval issue_category=trim(tostring(coalesce(issue_type, topic_category, "")))
| fields _time case_key_norm channel_canon ces_normalized issue_category ces_num
| sort 0 case_key_norm _time
| streamstats dc(channel_canon) AS channels_used BY case_key_norm
| eval channel_switch_signal=if(channels_used>=2, 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 case_key_norm [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-90d@d
    | eval _time=strptime(sys_updated_on, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | dedup sys_id sortby -_time
    | eval case_key_norm=upper(trim(number))
    | eval reassignment_n=coalesce(tonumber(reassignment_count), 0)
    | eval reopen_n=coalesce(tonumber(reopen_count), tonumber(incident_reopened_count), 0)
    | eval caller_key=tostring(coalesce(caller_id, u_caller_id, ""))
    | eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | eval resolved_epoch=strptime(coalesce(resolved_at, closed_at), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | eval handle_time_minutes=if(isnotnull(opened_epoch) AND isnotnull(resolved_epoch), round((resolved_epoch-opened_epoch)/60, 1), null())
    | eval issue_cat_sn=coalesce(category, "")
    | table case_key_norm reassignment_n reopen_n caller_key opened_epoch handle_time_minutes issue_cat_sn contact_type
  ]
| eval issue_category=coalesce(nullif(issue_category,""), issue_cat_sn)
| eval reopen_safe=coalesce(reopen_n,0)
| eval reassignment_safe=coalesce(reassignment_n,0)
| eval repeat_within_7d=if(reopen_safe>=1, 1, 0)
| eval long_handle_flag=if(handle_time_minutes>60, 1, 0)
| eval effort_index=round(ces_normalized*1.0 + reassignment_safe*0.5 + channel_switch_signal*1.0 + repeat_within_7d*0.7 + long_handle_flag*0.5, 1)
| stats avg(effort_index) AS avg_effort avg(ces_normalized) AS avg_ces_good dc(case_key_norm) AS cases sum(eval(channel_switch_signal)) AS switches sum(eval(repeat_within_7d)) AS repeats BY channel_canon issue_category
| sort - avg_effort
```

What the pipeline does:
‚ÄĘ **Scope** unions heterogeneous CES vendors ‚ÄĒ **`coalesce()`** shields field drift (`ticket_id` vs **`case_number`**).
‚ÄĘ **`ces_normalized`** implements documented inversion so dashboards align on **higher = easier** ‚ÄĒ subtitle dashboards accordingly.
‚ÄĘ **`sort` + `streamstats`** discovers **`channels_used`** only when multiple chronological rows exist per **`case_key_norm`** ‚ÄĒ augment with auxiliary **`ivr`**/**routing** sourcetypes when surveys emit singleton rows.
‚ÄĘ **`join type=left max=0`** mirrors **UC-23.7.1** join hygiene ‚ÄĒ prevents Splunk from silently truncating ties when CRM duplicates slip through.
‚ÄĘ **`repeat_within_7d`** uses **`reopen_n`** as pragmatic reopen proxy ‚ÄĒ swap to **`eventstats`-based intra-caller sequencing when **`caller_key`** fidelity supports longitudinal joins.
‚ÄĘ **`effort_index`** mixes perception + operational friction; treat weights as governance-owned numbers stored in **`effort_index_weights.csv`**.

Alerting clone: append `| where avg_effort>=7` after **30-day** window, throttle **channel_canon √ó issue_category**, email **VP Support** weekly digests.

**Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate**
Survey truth: compare **`stats count` / `avg(ces_num)`** slices to Qualtrics **Insights ‚Üí Distribution** or Medallia dashboard exports for identical question IDs. ServiceNow alignment: spot-check **`reassignment_count`** against **Performance Analytics ‚Üí Reassignment Rate** widgets. Join coverage: run `| stats count(eval(isnull(reassignment_n))) AS missing_join count AS survey_rows | eval pct_drop=round(100*missing_join/survey_rows,1)` ‚ÄĒ investigate when **`pct_drop>10`**. Five-ticket qualitative review: open ServiceNow **`number`** plus vendor response deep link for **`case_key_norm`** outliers.

**Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize**
Dashboard Studio layout: row 1 **`splunk.singlevalue`** rolling CES plus month-over-month delta bands. Row 2 **`splunk.heatmap`** **`avg_effort`**. Row 3 **`splunk.column`** CES by **`channel_canon`**. Row 4 prioritized table sorted by **`cases*avg_effort`** highlighting biggest savings opportunities. Cadence: weekly support leadership reads, monthly **VP Support + VP Customer Success + Product** joint review, quarterly board customer friction annex. Ownership: **VP Support** accountable, dotted line to **Chief Customer Officer**.

**Step 5 ‚ÄĒ Troubleshoot (CES- and survey-specific failure modes)**

1) **Polite-email vs direct-voice scoring bias** ‚ÄĒ Email respondents often compress variance (small standard deviation, inflated mean) because asynchronous norms discourage blunt negatives; voice callers articulate sharper disappointment even after comparable handling.

**Triage SPL:** `index=business sourcetype="qualtrics:response" earliest=-90d | stats avg(ces_score) AS avg stdev(ces_score) AS sd count BY channel_canon` ‚ÄĒ when **`sd`** on **`email`** collapses versus **`phone`** yet **`avg`** stays elevated, suspect measurement artifact.

**Mitigations:** publish CES bucket distributions (**low / mid / top** thirds), monitor **`survey_completion_rate`** per channel, blend **`avg_effort`** with operational **`reassignment_n`** before reallocating budgets.

2) **`case_number` ‚ÜĒ `number` identifier mismatch silently starving joins** ‚ÄĒ Qualtrics embedded data might strip **`INC`** prefixes while ServiceNow **`number`** retains them ‚ÄĒ **`join`** misses forty-plus percent of surveys despite healthy ingestion volume.

**Triage SPL:** compare `head 10` **`values(case_key)`** against **`values(number)`** from **`snow:incident`**.

**Mitigations:** defensive **`eval case_key_norm=if(match(case_key,"^[0-9]+$"),"INC".case_key,...)`** prior to **`join`**; compute **`pct_drop`** KPI weekly; alert beyond ten percent drift.

3) **`effort_index` coefficient drift versus lived renewal outcomes** ‚ÄĒ Default weights (**`ces_normalized`**, **`reassignment_n`**, **`channel_switch_signal`**, **`repeat_within_7d`**, **`long_handle_flag`**) assume homogeneous enterprises ‚ÄĒ regulated banking cohorts tolerate different reassignment norms than SMB SaaS tenants.

**Triage:** plot twelve-month **`effort_index`** quartiles versus **`renewal_flag`** / churn telemetry ‚ÄĒ weak **`R¬≤`** implies stale coefficients.

**Mitigations:** semi-annual logistic regression revisits weights; **`effort_index_weights.csv`** stores **`reviewed_date`** / **`reviewer`**; Dashboard Studio footer warns when **`now()-reviewed_epoch`** exceeds six months.

Secondary monitors: ExecProcessor retries on Qualtrics export jobs; **`_internal`** **`HttpInputDataHandler`** gaps on HEC tokens; **`snow:incident`** **`sys_updated_on`** stalls pointing at OAuth expiry identical to **UC-23.7.1** remediation paths.
grandmaExplanation: We blend quick survey answers about how hard getting help felt with ticket facts like transfers and repeats, so leaders see which kinds of problems through which channels annoy customers most.
